title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Here’s what happened in crypto today
briefing: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: EU crypto rulebook faces enforcement challenge as MiCA transition ends
summary: EU crypto rulebook faces enforcement challenge as MiCA transition ends
briefing: Lawyers and industry executives expect EU regulators to enforce MiCA differently as unauthorized crypto companies are required to wind down operations.

title: Upbit says it only expressed interest in future OUSD participation
summary: Upbit says it only expressed interest in future OUSD participation
briefing: Upbit said it only expressed interest in potentially joining the OpenStandard ecosystem in the future, as several South Korean firms distanced themselves from the OUSD initiative.

title: Belgian police arrest suspected phishing gang leader tied to $572K theft
summary: Belgian police arrest suspected phishing gang leader tied to $572K theft
briefing: Belgian authorities say a European phishing gang stole over $572,000 from victims before laundering the proceeds through cryptocurrency.

title: StanChart joins ESMA's first MiCA register update since deadline
summary: StanChart joins ESMA's first MiCA register update since deadline
briefing: ESMA publishes its first post-deadline MiCA register update, adding 37 crypto-asset service providers, including Standard Chartered and FalconX.

title: Bitcoin supply metric prints first 'buy' signal since late 2022 as bear market continues
summary: Bitcoin supply metric prints first 'buy' signal since late 2022 as bear market continues
briefing: Bitcoin supply dynamics pointed toward the end of the 2026 bear market after the first "buy" signal since November 2022, but analysis warned that BTC price could still go lower.

title: Zcash’s Ironwood upgrade faces possible delay over infrastructure readiness
summary: Zcash’s Ironwood upgrade faces possible delay over infrastructure readiness
briefing: Shielded Labs said exchanges, mining pools and wallets may need more time to complete Zcash’s Z3 software migration and safely prepare for Ironwood.

title: Defendant files to dismiss New York lawsuit seeking ownership of 39,069 Bitcoin wallets
summary: Defendant files to dismiss New York lawsuit seeking ownership of 39,069 Bitcoin wallets
briefing: A defendant who owns one of the dormant Bitcoin wallets filed to dismiss the New York case, which seeks ownership of $229 billion worth of Bitcoin considered lost.

title: American Charged in Israel With Spying for Iran in Exchange for Crypto
summary: American Charged in Israel With Spying for Iran in Exchange for Crypto
briefing: Israeli prosecutors charged a 21-year-old American man with spying for Iran while studying at a seminary in Jerusalem.

title: Farage Reported to UK Standards Watchdog Over Alleged Crypto Lobbying
summary: Farage Reported to UK Standards Watchdog Over Alleged Crypto Lobbying
briefing: A Labour MP says the Reform leader pressed the Bank of England on policy that could enrich his biggest donor, a major Tether investor.

title: Bitcoin ETFs Draw In $222M, Snapping 10-Day Losing Streak
summary: Bitcoin ETFs Draw In $222M, Snapping 10-Day Losing Streak
briefing: Thursday's inflows ended a run that drained $2.7 billion, but analysts warn one green day isn't necessarily a trend reversal.

title: OpenAI Offers US Government a $42 Billion Slice of Itself: Report
summary: OpenAI Offers US Government a $42 Billion Slice of Itself: Report
briefing: Sam Altman is pitching a 5% equity stake in OpenAI for the U.S. government—and reportedly wants every major AI company to do the same.

title: Russia Is Ready for 'Widespread Use' of Digital Ruble by September, Says Bank Governor
summary: Russia Is Ready for 'Widespread Use' of Digital Ruble by September, Says Bank Governor
briefing: Bank of Russia Governor Elvira Nabiullina said major banks and retailers are on track to begin accepting the digital ruble by September 1.

title: Treasury Department Sanctions Over 130 ISIS-Affiliated Crypto Wallets on Tron
summary: Treasury Department Sanctions Over 130 ISIS-Affiliated Crypto Wallets on Tron
briefing: More than 130 Tron wallets connected with a Central Asian ISIS affiliate were sanctioned by the U.S. government, with funds frozen by Tether.

title: AI Researchers Got Chatbots to Share Cocaine Recipes Using This One Wild Trick
summary: AI Researchers Got Chatbots to Share Cocaine Recipes Using This One Wild Trick
briefing: Researchers say a new jailbreak technique tricked AI models into treating attacker-written text as their own reasoning, bypassing safety guardrails and exposing a deeper security flaw.

title: Securitize Begins Trading on NYSE as Tokenized Shares Land on Solana, Avalanche
summary: Securitize Begins Trading on NYSE as Tokenized Shares Land on Solana, Avalanche
briefing: BlackRock-backed tokenization firm Securitize now has shares trading on the New York Stock Exchange—or via Solana and Avalanche.
